description: Strength is the capacity to exert force or continue while force presses back. The noun can concern a body, material, or a person's resolve. 'kema' (strong) describes the capacity in context, while 'kenua' (energy) is what remains available for action and may rise or fade without changing underlying strength.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge, then lower the tongue slightly into middle-height back rounded 'o'.
usage_notes: A possessor or preceding noun identifies whose or what strength is meant. The surrounding action should make clear whether the capacity is bodily, material, or inward.

semantic_domains.emotion: the inward capacity to continue under felt pressure
semantic_domains.physical: the capacity of a body or material to exert or withstand force
